name: Feedback Cover
description: The `feedback` cover platform allows you to create covers with position control that can optionally have feedback from sensors to detect the fully-open and fully-closed states (endstops), and from sensors to detect actual movement (opening/closing). In all the cases the current position is approximated with the time the cover has been moving in a direction.

config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name for the cover. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: Manually set the icon to use for the cover in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Update Interval
config_entries.description: The interval to publish updated position information to the UI while the cover is moving. Defaults to `1s`.

config_entries.label: Close Action
config_entries.description: The action that should be performed when the remote requests the cover to be closed.

config_entries.label: Close Duration
config_entries.description: The amount of time it takes the cover to close from the fully-open state.

config_entries.label: Close Endstop
config_entries.description: The ID of the [Binary Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/binary_sensor#config-binary_sensor) that turns on when the closed position is reached.

config_entries.label: Close Obstacle Sensor
config_entries.description: The ID of the [Binary Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/binary_sensor#config-binary_sensor) that turns on when an obstacle that blocks the close direction is detected.

config_entries.label: Close Sensor
config_entries.description:
**Set together — all of these must be set, or all left blank:** `open_sensor`, `close_sensor`.

The ID of the [Binary Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/binary_sensor#config-binary_sensor) that turns on when the cover is moving in the close direction.

config_entries.label: Open Action
config_entries.description: The action that should be performed when the remote requests the cover to be opened.

config_entries.label: Open Duration
config_entries.description: The amount of time it takes the cover to open up from the fully-closed state.

config_entries.label: Open Endstop
config_entries.description: The ID of the [Binary Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/binary_sensor#config-binary_sensor) that turns on when the open position is reached.

config_entries.label: Open Obstacle Sensor
config_entries.description: The ID of the [Binary Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/binary_sensor#config-binary_sensor) that turns on when an obstacle that blocks the open direction is detected.

config_entries.label: Open Sensor
config_entries.description:
**Set together — all of these must be set, or all left blank:** `open_sensor`, `close_sensor`.

The ID of the [Binary Sensor](https://esphome.io/components/binary_sensor#config-binary_sensor) that turns on when the cover is moving in the open direction.

config_entries.label: Stop Action
config_entries.description: The action that should be performed when the remote requests the cover to be closed or an endstop is reached.

config_entries.label: Device Class
config_entries.description: The device class for the sensor. See [https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/cover/#device-class](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/cover/#device-class) for a list of available options.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for code generation. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Acceleration Wait Time
config_entries.description: Considers a wait time needed by the cover to actually start moving after command is issued and takes it into account when computing cover position (useful for heavy covers with large inertia). Intended to not accumulate error when doing multiple partial open/close actions). The open/close duration includes one instance of this delay, as it is the total amount of time from issuing a command to reaching endstop. Defaults to `0s`.

config_entries.label: Assumed State
config_entries.description: Whether the true state of the cover is not known. This will make the Home Assistant frontend show buttons for both OPEN and CLOSE actions, instead of hiding or disabling one of them. Defaults to `true` if no sensor is available to known the actual state of the cover.

config_entries.label: Direction Change Wait Time
config_entries.description: Stops cover and forces a wait time between changes in direction, and takes it into account when computing cover position (useful to protect motors). When this option is set (even at 0s) if an open/close action is invoked while the cover is moving in the opposite direction, then and intermediate stop action will be invoked to generate the delay.

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, then this entity should not be added to any client's frontend, (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Entity Category
config_entries.description: The category of the entity. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default entity category.

config_entries.label: Has Built In Endstop
config_entries.description: Indicates that the cover has built in end stop detectors. In this configuration the `stop_action` is not performed when the open or close time is completed and if the cover is commanded to open or close the corresponding actions will be performed without checking current state. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Infer Endstop From Movement
config_entries.description: Whether to infer endstop state from the movement sensor. Requires movement sensors to be set, no endstop sensors and to have builtin endstops. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Only specifying an `id` without a `name` will implicitly set this to true.

config_entries.label: Max Duration
config_entries.description: The maximum duration the cover should be opening or closing. Useful for protecting from dysfunctional endstops. Requires internal, builtin or inferred endstops.

config_entries.label: MQTT Json State Payload
config_entries.description: When set to `true`, state changes will be published only to the `state_topic` as a single JSON object per state change. Example: ```json { "state": "open", "position": 100, "tilt": 50 } ``` When `false`, individual values are published to the `state_topic`, `position_state_topic`, and `tilt_state_topic` separately. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Obstacle Rollback
config_entries.description: The percentage of rollback the cover will perform in case of obstacle detection while moving. Defaults to `10%`.

config_entries.label: Position Command Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to receive cover position commands on.

config_entries.label: Position State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish cover position changes to. Not valid if `mqtt_json_state_payload` is set to `true`.

config_entries.label: Tilt Command Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to receive cover tilt commands on.

config_entries.label: Tilt State Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to publish cover cover tilt state changes to. Not valid if `mqtt_json_state_payload` is set to `true`.
